slides.title: Please and Thank You
slides.content:
Good manners are very important!

When we want something, we say 'please'.
When someone helps us, we say 'thank you'.

These two words make everyone feel happy and respected.
slides.tip: Please and thank you are the magic words!

slides.title: Being Polite
slides.content:
There are many ways to be polite:

Knock before entering a room - tap on the door first.
Don't grab things from others - ask first!
Say 'excuse me' or 'I'm sorry' when you make a mistake.

It is rude to take things without asking. Rude means not polite.

slides.title: Key Vocabulary
slides.content:
Please - a polite word when you ask for something
Thank you - a polite word when someone helps you
Knock - tap on the door before entering
Rude - not polite, bad manners
Grab - take something quickly (not polite!)
Thoughtful - kind, thinking about others
Pass - give something to someone at the table

slides.title: Can I...? vs May I...?
slides.content:
We use Can I...? and May I...? to ask for permission.

'Can I go to the toilet?' - this is correct and common.

'May I go to the toilet?' - this is more formal and polite.

Both are correct! But May I...? sounds more polite in formal situations.
slides.tip: Can I = everyday. May I = more polite and formal.

slides.title: Example: Can I / May I
slides.problem:
Choose Can I or May I for each situation:

1. Asking your teacher to leave the room.
2. Asking your friend to borrow a pen.
3. Asking the headteacher to use the phone.
steps: 1. Teacher (formal): MAY I leave the room, please?
2. Friend (informal): CAN I borrow your pen?
3. Headteacher (very formal): MAY I use the phone, please?
slides.answer: May I, Can I, May I

slides.title: Lunch at School
slides.html:
It was lunchtime at school. Tom was very hungry.

'Can I sit here?' Tom asked his friend Sara. 'Of course!' said Sara.

'Please pass the bread,' said Tom. Sara passed the bread. 'Thank you!' said Tom.

Then Max came and grabbed Sara's juice. 'Hey! That's rude!' said Sara. 'You should ask first!'

'I'm sorry,' said Max. 'May I have some juice, please?' 'Yes, you may,' said Sara with a smile. 'Thank you!' said Max. Being polite is easy!

slides.title: Polite with Technology
slides.content:
We should also be polite when using technology!

'Can I take your photo?' - always ask before taking a photo of someone.

'May I use the computer?' - ask before using shared devices.

Being thoughtful means thinking about others. Put your phone away when someone is talking to you!

slides.title: Example: Polite or Rude?
slides.problem:
Is this behaviour polite or rude?

1. Saying 'please' when you ask for something.
2. Grabbing a toy from your friend.
3. Knocking before entering a room.
4. Talking when the teacher is talking.
steps: 2. Grabbing a toy -> RUDE! You should ask first.

slides.title: The Thoughtful Friend
slides.html:
Sara was a very thoughtful girl. She always thought about her friends.

When Tom forgot his lunch, Sara said: 'Can I share my sandwich with you?' Tom smiled and said 'Thank you, Sara!'

When a new girl came to class, Sara knocked on her desk and said: 'May I sit with you?' The new girl was so happy!

Sara never grabbed things. She always said 'please'. She was never rude. Everyone loved Sara because she was kind and polite.

slides.title: Let's Review!
points: 🙏 Say 'please' when asking and 'thank you' when receiving
❌ Don't grab things - it's rude! Ask first.
💭 Be thoughtful - think about others' feelings
🗣️ Can I...? = everyday permission, May I...? = more formal and polite
💻 Be polite with technology too - ask before taking photos!
